set.body: Reorganize the project wiki so anyone — a new developer, a PM, an auditor — can find the right page and know whether it is current or historical. Adds a formal content model, stable page identities, truth-state banners, generated navigation and indexes, and an evidence chain from plans to releases. The full design lives in the plan doc; work proceeds in phases, foundations first.
set.title: IA & content model (supersedes wiki-information-architecture)

set.body: Turn the remaining warnings into hard CI failures, add sidebar/navigation rendering for GitLab, Azure DevOps, and Confluence wikis, and add a search command over the doc inventory.
set.title: Phase 5: promote gates to hard fail; platform render adapters (GitLab/ADO/Confluence); /worklog:find + glossary

set.body: Extend the item model beyond the fixed core (id/title/status/level/kind/milestone/priority) with a configurable optional-field set, so lightweight and heavyweight processes share one log. Config block (work_item_fields in .work/config.yml) enables fields per team; every enabled field carries a description so humans AND agents use it correctly; disabled fields never appear in prompts, forms, or validation. Recommended default-on: priority, estimate, risk, owner, milestone, acceptance_criteria, labels. Default-off: value, confidence, due_date, severity, time tracking. Dependencies stay typed (blocked_by/blocks, relates_to) and feed the Traceability Index. Design principles: small stable core; explicit documented options; conservative defaults; process-agnostic. Full spec discussed 2026-07-22; re-derive the detailed field table when this is planned.
set.title: Configurable work-item field model: optional fields (estimate, risk, effort, value, confidence, owner, due_date, acceptance_criteria, blocked_by/blocks) behind work_item_fields config
